##NAMES=Ruizite
##RRUFFID=R130787
##IDEAL CHEMISTRY=Ca_2_Mn^3+^_2_Si_4_O_11_(OH)_4_·2H_2_O
##LOCALITY=Wessels mine, Hotazel, Kalahari Manganese Field, Northern Cape Province, South Africa
##OWNER=RRUFF
##SOURCE=Marcus Origlieri
##DESCRIPTION=Brown prismatic crystals in radiating groups, with a globular gyrolite-like mineral, on and in hydroxyapophyllite-(K) with minor inesite.
##STATUS=The identification of this mineral is confirmed by single-crystal X-ray diffraction and chemical analysis
##URL=https://www.rruff.net/odr/rruff_sample/R130787
##MEASURED CHEMISTRY=(Ca_1.90_Sr_0.06_Mg_0.04_)_2.00_(Mn^3+^_1.88_Fe^3+^_0.07_Al_0.05_)_2.00_Si_4.01_O_11_(OH)_4_·2H_2_O
##DIFFRACTION SAMPLE DESCRIPTION=Single crystal, powder profile is calculated
##CELL PARAMETERS=a: 9.036 b: 6.1684 c: 11.9602 alpha: 90 beta: 91.433 gamma: 90 volume: 666.43 crystal system: monoclinic
##FILETYPE=Raman RAW
##RAMAN WAVELENGTH=780
